sourceMovie.title: Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory works as a family movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The family and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory clicked for you, Charlie and the Chocolate Factory (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: How the Grinch Stole Christmas
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory clicked for you, How the Grinch Stole Christmas (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. faq.question: What should I watch after Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory?
faq.answer: Try Charlie and the Chocolate Factory, How the Grinch Stole Christmas, Elf—each shares DNA with Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory?
faq.answer: Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory (1971) is best described as Family, Fantasy, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this family lane.
